name: Centralizer

consumes.minimum: 1 active comp + 1 selected layer (ShapeLayer with ≥1 path, TextLayer, or AVLayer)
consumes.description: Reads the active CompItem and its first selected layer. For ShapeLayer: traverses Contents to extract all ADBE Vector Group and ADBE Vector Shape-Group properties into a tree for UI display; user picks a path via the tree view, then reads that path's vertex points at the current time to compute bounding-box coordinates. For TextLayer: calls sourceRectAtTime to determine bounding rect. For AVLayer: reads layer.width, layer.height, layer.scale, and layer.position to derive bounds. Also reads layer.anchorPoint when adding anchor-center guides.

controls.name: Path Tree
controls.role: displays hierarchical shape groups and paths from the selected shape layer; user selects the target path to centralise

controls.name: Center
controls.role: when checked, adds two guides at the layer anchor point (vertical + horizontal center)

controls.name: BoundingBox
controls.role: when checked, adds four guides at the path bounding-box edges (minX, maxX, minY, maxY)

controls.name: BBox Center
controls.role: when checked, adds two guides at the computed path centroid (centerX, centerY)

controls.name: Proceed
controls.role: validates selection, adds slider-control effects with bound expressions to compute path geometry, harvests coordinate values, adds composition guides, then closes the dialog

controls.name: Cancel
controls.role: closes the dialog without adding any guides

functions: guard: alerts and exits when no active CompItem is present
guard: alerts and exits when no layers are selected in the active comp
guard: alerts and exits when a ShapeLayer contains no shape groups or paths
extract shape group and path hierarchy from ShapeLayer Contents via recursive traversal of ADBE Vector Group and ADBE Vector Shape-Group properties
display interactive tree-view palette for selecting a path within the shape layer hierarchy
build a fully-qualified AE expression path string for the user-selected path by walking the treeview selection upwards to the layer root
add six ADBE Slider Control effects (Center X, Center Y, Min X, Max X, Min Y, Max Y) to the shape layer

write a bounding-box + centroid expression onto each slider that reads the selected path's vertex points, accumulates group-level transform offsets, applies parent-layer toComp conversion, and dispatches the correct coordinate via thisProperty.propertyGroup(1).name
harvest evaluated slider values (post-expression) and adjust for layer scale
add composition-level guides based on checked options: center guides at anchor point, bounding-box guides at min/max X/Y, and bbox-center guides at computed centroid
handle TextLayer geometry via sourceRectAtTime and toComp projection for bounding-box calculation
handle AVLayer geometry via layer.width/height, scale, and position for bounding-box calculation
support KBar integration: run Center or BoundingBox operations headlessly from kbar.button.argument without showing the palette
wrap all guide-addition mutations in a single undo group named 'Add Guides' (or 'Add Center Guides' / 'Add Bounding Box Guides' in kbar mode)

scriptSim.scenario: success — AV layer selected, center and bounding box guides added
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Main Comp' (1920×1080, 24fps); single selected AV layer 'Footage Layer' at position [960, 540], scale [100, 100], source size 1920×1080; no kbar context; script runs non-interactively (AVLayer branch, no UI shown)

sim.note: Sweeps composition time from 0 to 5 seconds; output is the Center X coordinate of the selected path's bounding-box centroid as it changes with an animated path. The same expression body (differing only in the final sliderName dispatch) is applied identically to the Center Y, Min X, Max X, Min Y, and Max Y sliders.

notes: UI mode is declared DIALOG in @ui frontmatter; the showUI function creates Window('palette', ...) at runtime — the harness treats it as DIALOG per frontmatter.
The palette is only shown for ShapeLayer selections. TextLayer and AVLayer inputs bypass the UI entirely and immediately add guides.
Six ADBE Slider Control effects (Center X, Center Y, Min X, Max X, Min Y, Max Y) are added to the layer and remain after execution — the removal code is commented out in the source.
The same expression string is written to all six sliders; each slider self-identifies via thisProperty.propertyGroup(1).name to return the appropriate coordinate.

The expression accumulates group-level transform offsets by walking propertyGroup() levels and inspecting group.matchName; only groups where matchName !== 'ADBE Vector Group' contribute transform offsets.
When the shape layer has a parent, adjustedCenter and the individual axis coordinates are projected to composition space via thisLayer.toComp().
Harvested slider values are additionally scaled by layer.scale before being used as guide positions.
KBar integration: when kbar.button.argument is 'Center' or 'BoundingBox', the script runs headlessly on TextLayer/AVLayer without opening any UI.
addGuide is a CompItem method; it is not available in all AE versions — requires CS6 or later with guide support enabled.
The fixture success scenario covers the AVLayer non-kbar path only; ShapeLayer path requires AE runtime for treeview interaction and path vertex evaluation.
